title: Model map $g_1:(Q_1,\partial Q_1)\to(S^2\vee S^2,*)$ with $L(g_1)=1$ and its scaling laws

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The argument relies on a model map asserted with a citation only:

> "There is a piecewise smooth map $g_1:(Q_1,\partial Q_1)\to(S^2\vee S^2,*)$ with $L(g_1)=1$. This is a standard construction. For details, see \cite{GH} Prop 5.2."

together with the scaled version $g_{S_1}:(Q_{S_1},\partial Q_{S_1})\to(S^2\vee S^2,*)$ having $\Dil_1(g_{S_1})\lesssim1$ and $L(g_{S_1})=1$, and the normalization $\|\omega_i\|_{L^\infty}\lesssim S_1^{-2}$ for the radius-$S_1$ round metric.

**For a self-contained proof one must:**
1. Construct $g_1$ explicitly (e.g. $Q_1\to Q_1/\partial Q_1\cong S^3\xrightarrow{\eta}S^2\vee S^2$ realizing a Whitehead/linking product).
2. Compute $L(g_1)=1$ directly from the definition $\int_z\alpha_1\wedge g^*\omega_2$.
3. Verify the scaling: $\Dil_1(g_{S_1})\lesssim1$ under rescaling the cube to side $S_1$ and the spheres to radius $S_1$, and $\|\omega_i\|_{L^\infty}\lesssim S_1^{-2}$.

As written this is a citation-only assertion of a nontrivial existence + quantitative claim (F3).
